title: ferray-linalg: faer_bridge always copies — no zero-copy path for C-contiguous arrays
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

array2_to_faer() in faer_bridge.rs does 'let data: Vec<T> = a.iter().copied().collect()' then faer::Mat::from_fn which creates a second column-major copy. Every linalg operation copies the input data twice (once to flatten, once for faer's layout). NumPy's LAPACK bindings use the array's data pointer directly when the layout matches. faer supports faer::MatRef::from_column_major_slice for zero-copy access to column-major data. For C-contiguous ferray arrays, we could either: (1) use faer's row-major support if available, (2) transpose via stride tricks without copying, or (3) at minimum reduce to one copy instead of two.
